translated: The four-person version of Pop Talking.
It was so cute. And fun! Yeah.
It really was fun.
With Pop Talk-
ing, the lyrics move so fast from one person to the next, so the hand-offs are really quick.
It's like, "Oh, she's singing now! Now it's her turn!"
You don't have enough eyes to keep up, even when we usually sing it at lives.
But seeing it performed by just four people felt so fresh.
Because of that, they could walk side-by-side
and really look at each other's faces. It felt like four friends walking home together after school,
just chatting while they sang.
I really felt that.
I thought so too.
It was singing, but it felt more like a conversation. They'd end up laughing because of how the person before them sang.
Seeing them turn their real-time feelings into words like that was so fun to watch.
It felt like we were right there participating with them.
It was great to watch.
The intro to Pop Talking started with all four of them doing Kinako-chan's "Tekuteku Biyori" first.
They were all in matching outfits too.
The whole thing felt so special.
True.
Since they were in matching outfits, it really felt like they were heading home after club activities
or some other plans they had together.
Like, "Let's all go home!" It felt so much like high school girls.
I totally agree.
That was fun.
It was adorable.
Man, it was so good.
They looked like they were having the time of their lives.
They said they had a blast, and they were having so much fun backstage too.
I kind of wanted to join in.
That's awesome.
I was so jealous.
I want to sing it with all 11 of us again.
It's a song that really transforms and shines on stage.
This was the first time the chorus actually had choreography.
That's true.
We never had that before.
And they each showed their personality by doing peace signs or blowing kisses.
For me, I did a bit of a "gyaru" style peace sign.
They did peace signs that matched their respective characters.
I don't know if anyone noticed,
but there was a lot of individuality in those moments.
Wow, that's nice.
Yeah, that was really great.
They looked so happy.
Everyone, please listen to Pop Talking a lot!
Please do.
Alright, let's keep moving.
Next is from Liellaji Name: Ka-chan.
Thank you very much.
Thank you!
"I attended Day 2 of the 7th Live in Aichi."
"The song that left the biggest impression on me was Sing! Shine! Smile!."
"Just like the lyrics 'Sing, let's sing together, Smile, connecting our hearts'..."
"Singing this song with everyone made the whole venue feel like one. It was so much fun."
"It reminded me once again that Liella!'s music has so much power."
"Just like the name Yuigaoka suggests, I felt that we are all tied together through song."
"Through the 7th Live, I've come to love Sing! Shine! Smile! even more."
That's what they wrote.
Thank you so much.
Thank you!
We also have one from Liellaji Name: Nonokura Nonoka.
Thank you very much.
Thank you!
"Hearing that the 7th Live is recommended even for first-timers,"
"I attended my very first live on Day 2 of the Aichi performance."
Thank you!
Thanks for coming!
"I was completely captivated by everyone's charming performance,"
"and the three hours flew by in an instant."
"One thing that surprised me was during Sing! Shine! Smile!,"
"when the flow led to everyone singing together."
"At first, I was hesitant, wondering if it was okay to sing along instead of just doing calls."
"But the sense of unity from everyone singing the same song was so fresh and pleasant."
"I felt a connection with everyone in the venue, and it became my best memory."
"Great job on the 7th Live."
"I'll keep supporting you!"
That's the message.
Thank you so much.
